response: Arbutus is a genus of flowering plants in the family Ericaceae, native to warm temperate regions of the Mediterranean, western Europe, the Canary Islands, and North America. It is characterized by its 11 accepted species, which are found in warm temperate regions. Arbutus is also known as "A. unedo" in Latin. Aspidistra is a genus of flowering plants in the family Asparagaceae, subfamily Nolinoideae, native to eastern and southeastern Asia, particularly China and Vietnam. It is commonly known as "Aspidistra elatior" and is a foliage house plant that is very tolerant of neglect. Both Arbutus and Aspidistra are flowering plants.
